name: WhiteFiber, Inc.
business: WhiteFiber provides artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure solutions by owning high-performance computing (HPC) data centers and offering cloud-based GPU services for AI and machine learning developers. It also offers hosting and colocation services through Tier-3 data centers.

exposures.sentence: We own high-performance computing (HPC) data centers and provide cloud-based HPC graphics processing units (GPU) services, which we term cloud services, for customers such as AI application and machine learning (ML) developers (the HPC Business).
Our Tier-3 data centers provide hosting and colocation services.

In August 2024, we executed a binding term sheet with Boosteroid Inc. (Boosteroid), a global cloud gaming provider pursuant to which, we finalized initial orders of 489 GPUs, projected to generate approximately $7.9 million in contracted value in the aggregate through November 2029.

We expect our strategically placed WhiteFiber data centers in smaller urban areas, which we term edge data centers, will deliver carrier hotel-level connectivity, while our larger deployments will power AI-driven computing super-clusters, driving innovation and efficiency.

WhiteFiber is a provider of GPUs compute and purchases NVIDA H100, H200, B200, GB200 servers, as well as other servers, through OEMS, for example, Super Micro Computer Inc , Dell, and Hewlett Packard Enterprise.
Our MTL-2, MTL-3 and NC-1 projects are intended to feature direct-to-chip liquid cooling.
Finally, at certain new and existing sites, we intend to deploy natural gas fuel cell generation technology to increase available power and revenue potential.

catalysts.event: MTL-3 data center commenced billing Cerebras
catalysts.sentence: The site has commenced billing Cerebras as of November 1, 2025, in the amount of CAD 1.4 million (approximately 979 thousand USD) monthly for the duration of the five-year contract.
The option was exercised on January 14, 2026 and the purchase of MTL-3 is expected to close during the second quarter of 2026.
We intend to complete the first 27 gross MW (gross) of service at NC-1 in April 2026, with another 27 gross MW (gross) of service in May 2026.
We intend to complete the first 27 gross MW (gross) of service at NC-1 in April 2026, with another 27 gross MW (gross) of service in May 2026.
Management expects NC-1 to start generating revenue in June of 2026.

catalysts.event: Nscale billing commencement for 40 MW phase
catalysts.sentence: Billing for the 40 MW phase is expected to commence in June 2026, subject to completion of construction and commissioning.
On January 26, 2026, we completed a private offering of $230.0 million aggregate principal amount of 4.500% Convertible Senior Notes due 2031.

catalysts.event: WhiteFiber Iceland secured term loan facility
catalysts.sentence: On March 25, 2026, WhiteFiber Iceland ehf. (the Borrower), a subsidiary of the Company, entered into a secured term loan facility agreement with Landsbankinn hf, which provides for borrowings of up to $20 million.

catalysts.event: Cloud service order with 72 GB200 GPUs
catalysts.sentence: In March 2026, we entered into a service order with a new customer to provide services utilizing a total of 72 GB200 GPUs. The service order has an initial term of 12 months beginning on the services commencement date, after which it automatically renews for successive one-month periods unless terminated by either party. The deployment and revenue generation began on March 7, 2026.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. data center power demand expected to reach 298 GW by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: According to McKinsey & Company, power demand for data centers in the U.S. driven by the need for digital and AI capabilities is expected to reach 298 gigawatts by 2030, up from 60 gigawatts in 2024.

tamClaims.claim: Global data center market valued at $342B in 2023, expected to reach $622.4B by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: According to Prescient and Strategic Intelligence, Data-Center Market Size and Analysis, Trends, Drivers, Competitive Landscape and Forecast (2024-2030), the global data center market was valued at $342 billion in 2023 and is anticipated to reach $622.4 billion by 2030, expanding at a CAGR of 10.5% during 2024-2030.

tamClaims.claim: Global cloud AI infrastructure market forecast to grow from $60.5B in 2024 to $363.4B in 2030
tamClaims.sentence: The global cloud AI infrastructure market is forecasted to grow from $60.5 billion in 2024 to $363.4 billion in 2030, a compound annual growth rate of approximately 35%, according to research published by Mordor Intelligence.
